name: Fallen Souffle Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Bittersweet chocolate (or semisweet broken into pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. Line the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan with parchment paper.
3. Lightly butter and flour the paper.
4. Melt the chocolate and butter in a double boiler or metal bowl set over barely simmering water. Stir to combine, then remove from heat and set aside to cool.
5. In a bowl, combine the egg yolks with all but 1 teaspoon of sugar and beat at high speed until the mixture is light and forms a ribbon when the beaters are lifted from the bowl.
6. In a separate bowl, combine the egg whites with the reserved teaspoon sugar. Beat until the egg whites form soft peaks.
7. Fold one-third of the chocolate mixture into the yolk mixture. Fold in one-third of the egg whites. Repeat two more times so that all ingredients are incorporated.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 30 minutes. Do not overcook; the center should still be soft. Let the cake cool to room temperature for 3 hours. Do not remove from the pan sooner or it will fall apart.
9. Remove sides of springform pan and dust the cake with cocoa powder, then confectioners sugar, and again with cocoa and sugar.
10. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
11. Before serving, let the cake warm to room temperature and dust again with cocoa and confectioners sugar.
Note: For best results, use dark bittersweet chocolate like Lindt or Tobler; alternatively, semisweet chocolate can be used, reducing the sugar to 1/2 cup.
